title: Lymphatic system neuromodulation and uses thereof
abstract: Methods are disclosed for preventing systemic infection or controlling metastases or providing a vaccine adjuvant in a subject, the methods comprising stimulating one or more peripheral nerves innervating one or more lymph nodes of the subject.
claims: Claims What is claimed is: 1. A method of inhibiting systemic infection by infectious pathogens or controlling metastases or providing a vaccine adjuvant in a subj ect, the method comprising stimulating one or more peripheral nerves innervating one or more lymph nodes of the subject. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein stimulation is applied in an amount and manner effective to reduce antigen flow through lymph nodes. 3. The method of claim 1 or 2, wherein stimulation constrains infectious pathogens from traveling through the lymphatic system leading to systemic infection, wherein unconstrained travel leads to systemic infection. 4. The method of any of claims 1-3, comprising stimulating nerves innervating lymph nodes draining the site of an infection. 5. The method of any of claims 1-4, wherein nerve stimulation is applied after an injury to the skin of the subject, such as a large or deeply penetrating injury. 6. The method of any of claims 1-5, wherein the infectious pathogens are bacteria. 7. The method of any of claims 1 -6, wherein stimulating nerves innervating lymph nodes draining the site of a penetrating injury to the subject prevents bacteremia and sepsis. 8. The method of claim 1 or 2, wherein stimulation restricts tumor cells and micrometastases from traveling through the lymphatic system, wherein unrestricted travel leads to recolonization of cancer cells in forming a secondary tumor. 9. The method of claim 1, 2 or 8 comprising stimulating nerves innervating lymph nodes draining the site of a primary tumor. 10. The method of claim 1 or 2, wherein stimulation increases antibody production to an antigen present in a vaccine. 11. The method of any of claims 1-10, wherein femoral and sciatic nerves are stimulated in the popliteal fascia. 12. The method of any of claims 1-11, wherein electrical stimulation is applied using a monopolar or bipolar needle electrode, a cluster of penetrating electrodes, percutaneous electrical nerve stimulation (PENS), transcutaneous electrical nerve stimulation (TENS), or a chronically implanted nerve stimulator. 13. The method of any of claims 1-12, where electrical nerve stimulation is applied using 0.75 msec duration pulses at 2-20 Hz. 14. The method of any of claims 1-11, wherein magnetic nerve stimulation is applied using an electromagnetic coil creating a time-variable magnetic field. 15. The method of any of claims 1-15, wherein stimulation is applied for a duration of at least 1 minute. 16. The method of any of claims 1-15, wherein one session of electrical or magnetic stimulation is used.
